hooks.command:
# Guard: prevent npm imports of @tauri-apps/api (use window.__TAURI__ instead)
if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -q "from '@tauri-apps/api" 2>/dev/null || echo "$CONTENT" | grep -q 'from "@tauri-apps/api' 2>/dev/null; then
  echo '⚠️ ARCHITECTURE: This template uses window.__TAURI__ globals, not npm imports. Use window.__TAURI__.core.invoke() instead of importing from @tauri-apps/api. See .claude/skills/tauri-desktop-app/SKILL.md'
fi

# Guard: prevent window.close() in utility windows (use closeWindow() from window-utils.js)
if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -q 'window\.close()' 2>/dev/null; then
  echo '⚠️ DESKTOP PATTERN: Use closeWindow() from window-utils.js instead of window.close(). Browser APIs do not work in Tauri webviews.'
fi

# Guard: prevent browser dialogs in utility windows
if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE '\balert\(|\bconfirm\(|\bprompt\(' 2>/dev/null; then
  echo '⚠️ DESKTOP PATTERN: Do not use alert(), confirm(), or prompt(). These are browser dialogs. Use native Tauri dialogs or inline UI instead.'
fi

# Guard: prevent React/framework in utility windows
if echo "$FILE_PATH" | grep -q 'src/windows/' 2>/dev/null; then
  if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'import.*React|from .react.|createElement|useState|useEffect' 2>/dev/null; then
    echo '⚠️ ARCHITECTURE: Utility windows must be plain HTML + vanilla JS, not React/framework components. The workspace (index.html) is where the developer adds their framework.'
  fi
fi

# Guard: warn on pkill -f (can kill user processes)
if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -q 'pkill -f' 2>/dev/null; then
  echo '⚠️ CAUTION: pkill -f can kill unrelated user processes. Use specific process targeting or lsof -ti:PORT | xargs kill instead.'
fi
